name: org.machanism.machai:gw-maven-plugin
description: GW Maven Plugin 1.2.2-SNAPSHOT is a Maven plugin for integrating Machai Ghostwriter into Java project builds. Generated Javadoc describes Ghostwriter Maven Plugin as an advanced documentation automation tool for Java projects that automatically scans, analyzes, and assembles project documentation using embedded guidance tags and AI-powered synthesis. The plugin streamlines creation and maintenance of high-quality, consistent documentation and supports guided, AI-assisted updates directly from standard Maven goals. It is designed for repositories that keep instructions close to maintained files through embedded @guidance: blocks. During execution, it scans configured directories, gathers guidance instructions, resolves project context, and delegates file processing to the Ghostwriter engine. The documented plugin model supports both aggregator-style execution and per-module reactor execution for single-module documentation projects, multi-module Maven builds, and repository-wide content maintenance workflows that combine source code, documentation, and project site assets. Primary responsibilities documented in the Javadoc include exposing Maven goals for guided file processing and interactive act execution, scanning project content for embedded @guidance: instructions, forwarding shared configuration such as model selection, excludes, instructions, and credentials to the processing engine, integrating with Maven session, project, reactor, and settings metadata, providing class-discovery helper tools so Ghostwriter workflows can inspect project classes and dependencies, and cleaning temporary processing artifacts generated during guided workflows. Package org.machanism.machai.gw.maven contains Maven mojos and shared goal infrastructure: AbstractGWMojo centralizes shared configuration and scan execution, GWMojo implements the gw:gw aggregator guided processing goal, GWPerModuleMojo implements per-module guided processing within the reactor, ActMojo provides interactive or configured action execution, ActPerModuleMojo adapts act execution to standard reactor processing, and CleanMojo removes temporary processing artifacts. Package org.machanism.machai.gw.maven.tools provides helper types that expose Java class discovery and reflection metadata as function tools for Ghostwriter workflows; it scans a Maven project's compile and output classpaths, locates classes by simple-name pattern, and returns structured metadata such as modifiers, members, annotations, class origin paths, source file locations, and dependency artifact coordinates. Effective build metadata identifies packaging maven-plugin, project URL https://machai.machanism.org/gw-maven-plugin, Java release 8, Apache License 2.0, Maven Plugin Plugin 3.15.2, and direct runtime/provided dependencies including org.machanism.machai:ghostwriter, org.apache.commons:commons-lang3, com.google.guava:guava, org.apache.maven.shared:maven-shared-utils, org.apache.maven:maven-core, org.apache.maven.plugin-tools:maven-plugin-annotations, org.codehaus.plexus:plexus-interactivity-api, and org.slf4j:slf4j-api.

usageContext: Run Machai Ghostwriter guided file processing from Maven goals
Scan documentation trees such as src/site for embedded @guidance: blocks
Apply interactive or predefined action prompts over scanned files with gw:act
Process multi-module Maven reactors using aggregator traversal or standard reactor dependency ordering
Forward model selection, instruction sources, exclude patterns, input logging options, and GenAI credentials to the Ghostwriter engine
Resolve provider credentials from Maven settings.xml by using genai.serverId
Clean temporary artifacts created by generative-workflow document processing
Expose controlled Java class discovery and reflection metadata to AI-assisted workflows
targetEnvironment: Java 8 or later runtime compatible with Maven plugins
Documentation trees and project site assets containing embedded @guidance: instructions
integrations: Maven project compile and output classpaths

constructors.description: Creates the Maven goal implementation for gw:gw. Javadoc describes GWMojo as the Maven goal that runs Ghostwriter guided file processing for a project. The goal is aggregator-style, can run without a local pom.xml, and processes modules in reverse order with sub-modules handled before parent modules.

examples.description: How to use it: Step 1, install the ready-to-use Maven plugin by declaring org.machanism.machai:gw-maven-plugin:1.2.2-SNAPSHOT in pom.xml. Step 2, keep maintained documentation or site files under a scan root such as src/site and add embedded @guidance: blocks. Step 3, run mvn gw:gw so Maven creates org.machanism.machai.gw.maven.GWMojo and delegates guided updates to Ghostwriter.

How to use it: Step 1, configure provider credentials in ~/.m2/settings.xml under the server id CodeMie if authentication is required. Step 2, select the model and scan root from the command line. Step 3, invoke the plugin by full coordinates to run aggregator guided processing.

constructors.description: Creates the per-module guided processing goal. Javadoc describes GWPerModuleMojo as a Maven goal that processes documents for the current Maven module and executes once per module in the active reactor according to standard Maven dependency ordering.

examples.description: How to use it: Step 1, invoke the plugin from the execution root of a Maven reactor. Step 2, choose a module-local scan root such as src/site. Step 3, optionally supply shared instructions and exclude patterns. Step 4, let Maven run the goal once per module in normal reactor order.

constructors.description: Creates the interactive action goal implementation. Javadoc describes ActMojo as the Maven goal gw:act that runs an interactive, predefined action over a documentation tree. The action is a prompt applied to scanned documents and can be provided by configuration or collected interactively through Maven's Prompter when -Dgw.act is omitted.

examples.description: How to use it: Step 1, install or invoke the Maven plugin. Step 2, select the documentation tree with -Dgw.path. Step 3, provide a concrete action prompt with -Dgw.act. Step 4, run the action goal and inspect the prompt-driven updates.

How to use it: Step 1, run the action goal without -Dgw.act when interactive prompt collection is desired. Step 2, enter the requested action text through Maven's prompter. Step 3, let the plugin process scanned documents using the collected action.

constructors.description: Creates the per-module action goal implementation. Javadoc describes ActPerModuleMojo as the Maven goal gw:act-per-module that runs an action against the execution-root project using Maven's standard reactor build context.

examples.description: How to use it: Step 1, choose the execution-root directory of the Maven reactor. Step 2, provide an action prompt and scan path. Step 3, run the per-module action goal when action-based updates should align with standard reactor processing.

constructors.description: Creates the cleanup goal implementation. Javadoc describes CleanMojo as the Maven goal that deletes temporary artifacts created by Machai generative-workflow document processing and notes that it is typically bound to Maven's clean lifecycle.

examples.description: How to use it: Step 1, run guided or action-based Ghostwriter processing. Step 2, invoke gw:clean from the current project or module root. Step 3, allow org.machanism.machai.gw.maven.CleanMojo to remove temporary workflow artifacts.

constructors.description: Creates an empty helper instance for function-tool integrations. Javadoc states that projects must be registered later before tool invocations can resolve class information.

examples.description: How to use it: Step 1, create the helper when plugin runtime code needs function-tool support for project-aware class lookup. Step 2, register Maven projects before invoking lookup functions. Step 3, use the helper during AI-assisted workflows that need controlled classpath inspection.

constructors.description: Creates a helper instance backed by the supplied Maven project's classpath. Javadoc documents this constructor as using the provided Maven project to resolve classpath and output directories for later class discovery and reflective inspection.

examples.description: How to use it: Step 1, obtain the active org.apache.maven.project.MavenProject from plugin execution context. Step 2, construct org.machanism.machai.gw.maven.tools.ClassFunctionalTools with that project. Step 3, call documented lookup operations during Ghostwriter workflow execution.

constructors.description: Creates a holder for a single Maven project and eagerly initializes class discovery metadata. Javadoc states that ClassInfoHolder builds a dedicated java.net.URLClassLoader from the project's compile classpath together with main and test output directories, then records physical locations and artifact coordinates for public and protected classes visible from that classpath.

examples.description: How to use it: Step 1, create the holder with the active org.apache.maven.project.MavenProject. Step 2, allow it to scan compile and output paths eagerly. Step 3, reuse the holder for class lookup, source-path resolution, classpath-origin lookup, and dependency-artifact identification inside AI-assisted workflows.

features.name: gw:gw
features.description: Aggregator goal for guided file processing. Javadoc documents GWMojo as the Maven goal that runs Ghostwriter guided file processing for a project, can run without a local pom.xml, and processes modules in reverse order with sub-modules before parent modules.

examples.description: How to use it: Step 1, place embedded @guidance: blocks in files you want Ghostwriter to maintain. Step 2, run the aggregator goal from a project or target directory. Step 3, review the updated files after guided processing completes.

How to use it: Step 1, enable multi-threaded module processing with -Dgw.threads=true when desired. Step 2, limit scanning to src/site and turn on input logging. Step 3, inspect the logged inputs and generated updates.

features.name: gw:gw-per-module
features.description: Reactor-oriented guided processing goal. Javadoc describes GWPerModuleMojo as processing documents for the current Maven module and running once per module in the active Maven reactor while Maven determines module execution order from the standard dependency graph.

examples.description: How to use it: Step 1, start from the execution root of the Maven build. Step 2, restrict scanning to a module documentation directory such as src/site. Step 3, let Maven execute the goal once per module in dependency order.

How to use it: Step 1, provide shared instruction locations and exclude patterns. Step 2, resolve credentials from settings.xml through a Maven server id. Step 3, run the goal so each module is processed with consistent workflow configuration.

features.name: gw:act
features.description: Interactive action goal. Javadoc documents ActMojo as a goal that runs an interactive, predefined action over a documentation tree, where the action is a prompt applied to scanned documents and can be provided directly or collected interactively through Maven's Prompter.

examples.description: How to use it: Step 1, choose a concrete editing task such as rewriting headings. Step 2, point the goal at the desired scan root. Step 3, run the goal and inspect updated documentation generated from the action prompt.

How to use it: Step 1, prepare a directory containing predefined action definitions. Step 2, pass it through -Dgw.acts and enable input logging if needed. Step 3, run the goal so predefined actions can be used with scanned documents.

features.name: gw:act-per-module
features.description: Per-module action goal. Javadoc describes ActPerModuleMojo as running an action against the execution-root project using Maven's standard reactor build context.

examples.description: How to use it: Step 1, select the reactor execution root. Step 2, provide a prompt for the change you want to apply. Step 3, run the goal to execute action-driven updates while following standard reactor processing.

features.name: gw:clean
features.description: Cleanup goal that removes temporary artifacts created during Machai generative-workflow document processing. Javadoc notes that this goal is typically bound to Maven's clean lifecycle.

examples.description: How to use it: Step 1, complete one or more guided or action-based processing runs. Step 2, invoke the cleanup goal from the project or module root. Step 3, verify that temporary workflow files have been removed.

features.name: ClassFunctionalTools.findClass
features.signature: public java.lang.String findClass(java.lang.String className, java.io.File projectDir)
features.description: Finds fully qualified class names whose simple names match the supplied regular expression. Javadoc describes ClassFunctionalTools as providing function-tool integrations for discovering Java classes from the classpath of one or more Maven projects.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.gw.maven.tools.ClassFunctionalTools tools = new org.machanism.machai.gw.maven.tools.ClassFunctionalTools(project);
java.lang.String matches = tools.findClass(".*Mojo", new java.io.File("."));
examples.description: How to use it: Step 1, create the helper with an active org.apache.maven.project.MavenProject or register projects first. Step 2, call findClass with a simple-name regular expression such as .*Mojo. Step 3, use the returned fully qualified class names inside workflow logic that needs class discovery.

features.name: ClassFunctionalTools.getClassInfo
features.signature: public java.util.Map<java.lang.String, java.lang.Object> getClassInfo(java.lang.String className, java.io.File projectDir)
features.description: Returns reflective information for a specified class. Javadoc states that tools in this package return structured metadata such as modifiers, members, annotations, class origin paths, source file locations, and dependency artifact coordinates for AI-assisted workflows.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.gw.maven.tools.ClassFunctionalTools tools = new org.machanism.machai.gw.maven.tools.ClassFunctionalTools(project);
java.util.Map<java.lang.String, java.lang.Object> info = tools.getClassInfo("org.machanism.machai.gw.maven.GWMojo", new java.io.File("."));
examples.description: How to use it: Step 1, construct the helper with a registered Maven project context. Step 2, request reflective information for a fully qualified class name. Step 3, forward returned structured metadata to the surrounding AI-assisted processing logic.

features.name: ClassInfoHolder
features.description: Project-scoped class discovery holder. Javadoc documents that ClassInfoHolder builds a dedicated java.net.URLClassLoader from the project's compile classpath together with main and test output directories, scans visible classes, and records physical locations and artifact coordinates for public and protected classes loadable from that classpath.

examples.description: How to use it: Step 1, initialize the holder with the current org.apache.maven.project.MavenProject. Step 2, let it build class discovery metadata eagerly. Step 3, reuse the holder for class lookup, source-path resolution, classpath-origin lookup, and artifact identification during workflow execution.

customizations.name: gw.model
customizations.description: Provider or model identifier forwarded to the Ghostwriter engine. Effective build metadata defines gw.model as CodeMie:gpt-5.5-2026-04-24, and the Javadoc describes model selection as a shared configuration concept for selecting a specific GenAI backend.

examples.description: Select the provider and model identifier to be used by Ghostwriter during guided execution.

customizations.name: gw.path
customizations.description: Configurable scan root used to determine where files containing @guidance: blocks are searched. Javadoc examples commonly use src/site as the documentation tree.

examples.description: Restrict workflow scanning to a module documentation directory rather than a broader project tree.

customizations.name: gw.instructions
customizations.description: Additional instruction source locations that complement embedded guidance blocks during document processing.

examples.description: Add a shared instruction source for the guided processing workflow.

customizations.name: gw.excludes
customizations.description: Exclude patterns used to skip generated folders, dependencies, or other irrelevant paths while scanning documentation or project content.

examples.description: Exclude generated or unrelated directories from scanning so processing stays focused on intended content.

customizations.name: genai.serverId
customizations.description: Maven settings.xml server identifier used to resolve provider credentials. Package Javadoc documents that when -Dgenai.serverId is set, the plugin reads the matching <server> entry from ~/.m2/settings.xml and forwards credentials or additional configuration fields to the workflow. Effective build metadata defines the default value CodeMie.

examples.description: Reuse credentials stored in standard Maven settings.xml so the workflow can authenticate to the selected GenAI backend without embedding secrets in project files.

customizations.name: logInputs
customizations.description: Boolean flag for logging the list of workflow input files, useful when troubleshooting or auditing scanned content.

examples.description: Enable logging of scanned input files during action-based processing.

customizations.name: gw.threads
customizations.description: Aggregator guided processing option for enabling or disabling multi-threaded module processing with gw:gw.

examples.description: Enable multi-threaded module processing when using the aggregator guided goal.

customizations.name: gw.act
customizations.description: Action text or prompt to apply with gw:act or gw:act-per-module. Javadoc states that gw:act can prompt interactively when an action is not supplied through configuration.

examples.description: Provide a direct command-line prompt for one-off action-driven documentation changes.

customizations.name: gw.acts
customizations.description: Optional location containing predefined action definitions. Effective build metadata defines gw.acts as https://raw.githubusercontent.com/machanism-org/machai/refs/heads/main/acts for a reusable acts catalog location.

examples.description: Point the action goal at a local directory containing predefined action definitions.
Use a remote acts catalog location when workflows should resolve shared predefined actions.

examples.title: Install, configure, and run guided documentation processing
examples.scenario: Use the artifact as a ready-to-use Maven plugin for guided Ghostwriter updates over files containing embedded @guidance: blocks.
steps: Step 1: Add org.machanism.machai:gw-maven-plugin:1.2.2-SNAPSHOT to the build/plugins section of pom.xml.
Step 2: Prepare maintained files, commonly under src/site, and add embedded @guidance: blocks where guided processing should occur.
Step 3: If your selected GenAI backend requires credentials, add a matching <server> entry to ~/.m2/settings.xml and reference it with -Dgenai.serverId=CodeMie.
Step 4: Choose the model with -Dgw.model and the scan root with -Dgw.path.
Step 5: Run gw:gw from the project root and review updated files after execution.
examples.description: This practical how-to scenario shows how to install the Maven plugin, configure provider credentials through Maven settings.xml, define the scan root and model, and run guided Ghostwriter processing from a standard Maven command.

examples.title: Run a predefined or interactive action over documentation files
examples.scenario: Use the action goal to apply a prompt-driven transformation to scanned documentation content.
steps: Step 1: Ensure org.machanism.machai:gw-maven-plugin:1.2.2-SNAPSHOT is declared in pom.xml or invoke it by full coordinates.
Step 2: Decide which directory to scan, for example src/site.
Step 3: Provide a prompt with -Dgw.act for configured execution, or omit it to let the plugin collect the prompt interactively.
Step 4: Optionally point -Dgw.acts to a directory or catalog of predefined action definitions.
Step 5: Run the goal and inspect the resulting updates before committing them.
examples.description: This example demonstrates practical ways to use the ready-to-run action goal: supplying a prompt directly, pointing to predefined action definitions, or entering the action interactively during execution.

examples.title: Process a multi-module build in per-module reactor mode
examples.scenario: Use the per-module guided processing goal when standard Maven reactor dependency ordering is preferred over aggregator reverse-order traversal.
steps: Step 1: Open the execution-root directory of the Maven reactor build.
Step 2: Optionally configure credentials in ~/.m2/settings.xml if the selected model backend requires them.
Step 3: Run gw:gw-per-module with a scan root such as src/site.
Step 4: Add instruction locations and exclude patterns if needed for shared workflow control.
Step 5: Review each module's results after the reactor build completes.
examples.description: This how-to scenario shows when to choose the per-module guided goal so processing aligns with Maven reactor ordering while still using Ghostwriter integration.

examples.title: Use class discovery helpers during AI-assisted plugin workflows
examples.scenario: Create the documented tools-package helpers so plugin workflows can inspect project and dependency classes in a controlled way.
steps: Step 1: Obtain the active org.apache.maven.project.MavenProject from plugin execution context.
Step 2: Create org.machanism.machai.gw.maven.tools.ClassInfoHolder or org.machanism.machai.gw.maven.tools.ClassFunctionalTools for that project.
Step 3: Use the holder or helper to discover matching classes, inspect reflective metadata, or resolve classpath origins and source paths.
Step 4: Pass structured metadata into the surrounding Ghostwriter workflow when class-awareness improves AI-assisted processing.

code.description: This example shows how documented helper classes can be created and used to obtain structured reflective metadata for a project class during workflow execution.

examples.description: This practical scenario explains how the tools package supports AI-assisted workflows that need controlled introspection of project and dependency classes.

examples.title: Clean temporary Ghostwriter workflow artifacts
examples.scenario: Run the maintenance goal that removes temporary files created during guided or action-based workflows.
steps: Step 1: Execute one or more processing goals such as gw:gw or gw:act.
Step 2: Run gw:clean from the current project or module root.
Step 3: Verify that temporary workflow artifacts have been removed from the working tree.
examples.description: This example provides a straightforward how-to workflow for removing temporary files created by Ghostwriter processing.
